title: festuca ovina subsp. halleri
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. halleri, also known as Haller's fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. halleri include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations and respiratory problems.
